task: Diagnosed and fixed three critical bugs in activity tracking, handover generation, and session continuity that were causing false inactivity alerts, incorrect quota announcements, and unwanted session auto-launches; identified that session continuity via stop hook does not preserve stage-plan context when autonomous handover jobs run between user interactions.
discussion_summary: The askr system had three interconnected bugs: (1) idle trigger (Trigger C) was being announced as a quota emergency and auto-launching new sessions, (2) the idle threshold was measuring time since last completed turn rather than actual user inactivity, and (3) session continuity via the stop hook was not preserving stage-plan context when autonomous jobs ran between user interactions. This session confirmed the user's clarification that the continuity problem extends beyond mid-session companion opens to include autonomous handover jobs that run after context-switch stops—when a multi-step stage plan is interrupted mid-implementation, the autonomous job (e.g., git save, test runner) has no knowledge of remaining stages. Root causes traced to lifecycle.py's code-path reuse across different trigger types (quota, context, idle) causing semantic mismatches, and to checkpoint.py's _build_transcript_text() stripping tool inputs that would preserve plan context.

accomplishments.what: Confirmed three separate bugs in idle trigger, idle threshold measurement, and session continuity via stop hook
Identified root cause: _build_transcript_text() in checkpoint.py strips all tool input except Write/Edit/MultiEdit/Bash, losing plan context
Traced idle trigger false announcements to code-path reuse between quota/context/idle trigger types in lifecycle.py
Documented that autonomous handover jobs (via stop hook) lack visibility into multi-stage plans interrupted mid-implementation

in_progress.what: Preserve full tool input context in _build_transcript_text() to maintain stage-plan visibility across autonomous handover jobs; enforce token limits via API call parameters, not post-generation truncation
Decouple idle trigger semantics from quota/context trigger code paths; ensure idle conditions do not trigger session auto-launch or quota announcements

next_actions.action: Modify _build_transcript_text() in checkpoint.py to preserve full tool input for all block types, not just Write/Edit/MultiEdit/Bash; this restores stage-plan context in autonomous handover jobs
next_actions.why: Current stripping loses the user's multi-step plan when autonomous job runs between context-switch stops; full context is needed for next Claude session to understand remaining stages

next_actions.action: Enforce token limits via max_tokens parameter in call_claude() rather than post-generation truncation; if token enforcement is not possible, require Claude to wrap required outputs in <required></required> tags
next_actions.why: User rejected mindless truncation of LLM outputs; truncating after generation wastes compute and loses semantic structure; enforcement at call time is cleaner

next_actions.action: Audit all call_claude() invocations to ensure max_tokens is set appropriately and no post-response truncation occurs
next_actions.why: Establishes baseline for token-limit enforcement; prevents future truncation bugs

next_actions.action: Test autonomous handover job flow with multi-stage plan: implement stage 1-4, trigger context-switch stop, verify autonomous job preserves remaining stages in handover for next session
next_actions.why: Validates that session continuity bug is fixed; confirms stage-plan context survives autonomous job boundary

decisions.decision: Historical billed entries (pre-OAuth) retain cost_usd for audit trail; new OAuth entries use quota_five_hour_pct only
decisions.reason: Maintains backward compatibility with existing cost records while accurately reflecting OAuth's data limitations

decisions.decision: Idle trigger uses dedicated _checkpoint_idle_inactivity() function, not generic _execute_trigger() shared with quota/context triggers
decisions.reason: Idle semantics (user absence from app) differ fundamentally from quota/context semantics (resource exhaustion); code-path reuse caused false announcements and unwanted session launches

decisions.decision: Voice broadcast fires only on emergency conditions (quota/context at 90%+), never per-turn
decisions.reason: Per-turn announcements create spam and false urgency; emergencies are rare and warrant interruption

decisions.decision: ask CLI uses separate API key, not Claude Code's OAuth token
decisions.reason: Keeps CLI and Claude Code authentication domains separate; prevents quota/cost confusion between interactive and CLI usage

user_rejected_decisions.what_was_proposed: Truncate LLM outputs based on character or token limits after generation
user_rejected_decisions.user_signal: no sort of manual truncation must be done, any truncation to LLM outputs will not be allowed, if we need truncated responses, let's enforce it via the calls

relational_files.why: Defines idle trigger logic and _execute_trigger() code path that was causing false announcements
Contains _build_transcript_text() that strips tool inputs, losing stage-plan context in autonomous handover jobs
Records architectural decisions about trigger semantics and API authentication

blockers: Session continuity bug requires changes to both checkpoint.py (preserve tool input) and lifecycle.py (decouple idle trigger); both must be coordinated to avoid regressions
